reasoning: Attacked on 'is it truly a bytecode VM or a jump table the auditor over-read' — and it is unambiguously a per-fighter bytecode VM. I read the dispatcher myself. CHNXT (WSCPU.MAC:829-833) fetches from a PER-ALIEN program counter `LDU A$CHPC(X) / LDA 0(U) / STA A$CHOP(X)`; CHOPDO (835-845) decodes `LDA A$CHOP(X) / ANDA #7 / LSLA / LDU #TCHOP / JMP @A(U)` — textbook opcode dispatch through a jump table. TCHOP (862-868) is exactly the opcode set the finding names: 0:IF (CHIF.D), 1:GOTO (CHGO.D, rewrites A$CHPC), 2:GOSUB (CHGS.D, saves return addr to A$CHRT), 3:RETURN (CHRT.D, restores A$CHPC from A$CHRT), 4/5:TWIRL (CHTW.D/E), 6/7:CHERR (SWI). That is a program counter + a one-deep call stack + conditional/branch opcodes = a VM, not a state machine. The script bytecode is emitted by the .CUNTIL/.CIF/.CGOTO/.CGOSUB/.CRETURN/.CT macros (931-967); `.CT 40,0,C$MF ;TAKES ME HALFWAY TO PLAYER` at WSCPU.MAC:1331 is verbatim. Event gates are real: A$CHCN control mask tested against A$CHST status (836-840), events C$AS (player in sights, set at 620), C$PN, C$AG, C$AH, C$PS, and the random-split C$R1/C$R2 (seeded from P.RND1 at 534-535). SPLIT (1605) is a genuine .CGOSUB'd subroutine with C$R1/C$R2 branching. Our moveEnemy (sim.ts:1109-1148) is a hand 2-state machine: approach (homing + swoop bank) then a single per-fighter `peeling` latch — no PC, no opcodes, no event masks, no subroutines, no scripts. state.ts:276 itself concedes 'the full ... per-fighter script VM (§5.1/§5.3) is deferred.' Genuine structural SIMPLIFICATION; class DIVERGENCE / fix / size l all correct.
corrections: Script-count precision: the finding says '~13 distinct scripts (TCH1A1…TCH1DZ)'. The TCH1 family actually holds 16 labels — 4 groups (A/B/C/D) × 4 scripts each (x1, x2, x3, and the xZ 'BE MEAN TO PLAYER' loiter loop): TCH1A1/A2/A3/AZ, 1B*, 1C*, 1D*. On top of those sit 12 TCH2* entry points (TCH2A1…TCH2D3) that each `.CGOSUB SPLIT` then fall through into the matching TCH1 script, plus the SPLIT subroutine — so the true authored surface is ~28 entry points over 16+ script bodies, not ~13. This makes the VM strictly LARGER than the finding states; it does not weaken the finding or change the size-l rating.

reasoning: The factual core — ROM has no TIE-body↔player collision; ours does — is true, and I confirmed it three independent ways. (1) Primary source: WSCPU's COLLISION section (341-446) contains only CPHTSA 'SPACE LAZAR HIT ALIEN SHIP' — the PLAYER's laser decrementing A$HTA on the alien (362 `DEC A$HTA(X)`), leading to XPSA. A grep of WSCPU for damage/shield/hit shows the only damage counter is A$DMC on the ALIEN (361) and the only 'SHIELDS' reference (634) is the alien's own glow-delay, never player damage. There is no reverse alien-body-vs-ship test anywhere in the alien code. (2) The choreography never bee-lines into the cockpit: every script terminates in an infinite `.CGOTO 10$` loiter loop (TCH1AZ:1387, 1BZ:1458/1471, 1CZ:1526/1529, 1DZ:1649/1653/1656) and CPCHKL clamps the alien to ±$7CFF (799-811). (3) Our own state.ts:296-297 admits it: 'the model itself has no body collision, but the story deliberately keeps it.' Our sim.ts:308-315 charges a shield when a TIE reaches COCKPIT_HIT_RADIUS (`damage++`, player-death cause 'enemy'). Divergence real; class DIVERGENCE correct.
corrections: Recommendation must reconcile with G-010, not stand as an independent 'fix'. A-010 and pair-guns G-010 describe the IDENTICAL behaviour and the identical code (sim.ts:308-315) with opposite dispositions (A-010: fix/remove; G-010: accept). Per coverage-review.md §2b and the code's own record, the correct disposition is ACCEPT: it is a documented, deliberate product deviation (story 9-3 AC#3, state.ts:288-299 'the story deliberately keeps it'), and A-011's per-fighter peel means only a near-dead-center TIE — one whose lateralOffset is inside COCKPIT_HIT_RADIUS=80 (sim.ts:1120) — ever reaches the cockpit sphere at all, so it rarely triggers. The divergence is genuine and worth RECORDING, but A-010 should be demoted to a cross-reference of G-010's accept rather than shipping a conflicting size-m removal task. The ROM fact is CONFIRMED; the 'fix' recommendation is the part that is wrong.

reasoning: Two genuinely different lifecycles, both cited correctly. ROM: a TIE never peels per-fighter during combat — every choreography script loops forever in a 'BE MEAN TO PLAYER' loiter (`.CGOTO 10$`, e.g. WSCPU.MAC:1387/1458/1526/1649). Retreat is a WAVE-END event: CPURET (WSCPU.MAC:468) recentres each survivor's Y/Z toward the Death Star and drives depth `ADDD #400 ;RACE BACK TO DETH STAR` (500) per frame until M$TX overflows and A$TYP is cleared (503-514). I verified CPURET is NOT per-combat-frame: its only caller is PHESP2 (WSMAIN.MAC:1460, `JSR CPURET ;COMPUTER DRONES RUN AWAY` at 1473) — the space-descent exit phase. Unit check: $400=1024 (ground-truth ×$400 lateral/step unit), 1024 × 20.508 = 20,999.9 ≈ 21,000 u/s toward the Death Star — the finding's number is right and used ×20.508, not ×60. Ours (sim.ts:1120): each fighter independently latches `peeling` when `dist <= TIE_NEAR_BOUND(2048) && lateralOffset >= COCKPIT_HIT_RADIUS(80)`, then thrusts outward and is culled at TIE_EXIT_RANGE (sim.ts:207). So the screen empties continuously, per-fighter, not en masse at wave end. Divergence real; class DIVERGENCE / accept correct (our peel is a reasonable stand-in that also prevents the full-frame-wall defect; a faithful version couples to the space-phase timer, tracked under A-009/A-019). No error found.

The ROM fire decision is a gated bundle exactly as described, and ours is a deterministic seconds cooldown missing most of it. I traced CPUAL: fire requires (a) player-in-view `BITA #C$PV/100 / BEQ 40$` (WSCPU.MAC:624-625, 'NO SHOOTING GUNS IF PLAYER CANT SEE US'), (b) not aiming-in-front C$T9 (627-628), (c) range past the floor `LDD M.XP / SUBD #800 / BLE 40$` (629-631), (d) not glowing/being-hit `LDA A$GLW(X) / BNE 40$` (632-633), then (e) a FREE-RUNNING FRAME-COUNTER mask `LDA FRAME+1 / ANDA 0(U) ;SPACE THEM OUT / BNE 40$` (646-648) — not a reload timer — and (f) a PRNG threshold `LDA P.RND1 / CMPA 1(U) / BLS 40$` (649-651). The mask/threshold come from TGPROB row WV.HRD: wave 0/1 use `.PROB 0F,080,x` (738-739) = mask $0F (fire ≤1/16 frames) gated ~50% by threshold $80 (P.RND1 > 128 = 127/256 ≈ 49.6%). Masks tighten $0F→$07→$03 (1-in-16/8/4) with hardness; verified against the 11-row table. Cadence math: 16/20.508 ≈ 0.78 s window ÷ ~0.5 hit-rate ≈ ~1.5 s effective wave-0/1 cadence — the finding's number, using ×20.508. Ours (sim.ts:236): `inPassWindow && cooldown <= 0 && enemyShots.length < maxConcurrentShots`, cooldown reset to the scalar ENEMY_FIRE_INTERVAL=1 (state.ts:232); inPassWindow (235) is only `!peeling && length(pos) > TIE_NEAR_BOUND` — a RANGE gate, no in-view/arc test, no frame-mask, no PRNG. The missing in-arc requirement is real (our TIEs can fire with the cockpit outside their view frustum). The slot-cap half IS ported faithfully (A-007). Divergence real; class DIVERGENCE / accept correct. No error found.

Verified both sides. ROM difficulty is a data-driven index, not a scalar. WV.HRD = min(SP.WAV + GM.DIF, 15): WSMAIN.MAC:1358-1364 `STA SP.WAV / ADDA GM.DIF ;INCLUDE OVERALL DIFFICULTY / CMPA #15. / IFHI / LDA #15. / STA WV.HRD ;CURRENT WAVES DIFFICULTY` — the finding's formula is exact (15. is decimal per trailing period). WV.HRD then selects a TGPROB row for the fire gate (WSCPU.MAC:635-644, A-012) and enemy SPEED/maneuvers come from the choreography scripts' C$MF/C$MF2 move-flags (A-014) and TSPWAV composition (A-017), never a multiplier. Ours (gameRules.ts:190-199): `const ramp = 1 + (wave - 1) * RAMP_PER_WAVE` with RAMP_PER_WAVE = 0.15 (165), scaling spawnInterval, enemySpeed and enemyFireInterval by one scalar. gameRules.ts:139-147 labels the ramp 'authentic-FEEL', single-sourced — invented, not ported. The finding is careful to exclude fire-concurrency (A-007, ported 1:1) and correctly says only the scalar enemySpeed/spawnInterval ramp has no ROM counterpart (the fire cadence does have a data-driven TGPROB analog, just not ported as such). Divergence real; class DIVERGENCE / accept correct. No error found.

The flagged shift-arithmetic trap was the whole point of this one, and I decoded it from the definitions rather than trusting the mnemonic. ASRD (MOP69.MAC:49-52) = `ASRA / RORB` = one SIGNED 16-bit arithmetic-shift-right of D (÷2, sign-preserving via ASRA feeding carry into RORB). The numbered routines are a fall-through chain (WSMATH.MAC:62-67): `.IRP .A <8,7,6,5,4,3,2,1> / ASRD'.A':: / ASRA / RORB / .ENDR / ASRD0:: RTS`. So `JSR ASRD6` enters at ASRD6 and executes ASRA/RORB six times (ASRD6→5→4→3→2→1) then RTS = ÷64; `JSR ASRD5` = five times = ÷32. Verified, not assumed. SNMVF (WSCPU.MAC:224-237) builds velocity = (M$AX,M$BX,M$CX)÷64 — the object's unit forward axis (magnitude $4000) scaled down: $4000÷64 = $100 = 256 units/frame (C$MF). SNMVF2 (209-222) uses ASRD5: $4000÷32 = $200 = 512 units/frame (C$MF2). At the ground-truth 20.508 Hz: 256 × 20.508 = 5,250 u/s and 512 × 20.508 = 10,500 u/s — the finding's figures exactly, and it explicitly rejected the 60-fps trap (512×60 = 30,720 ≈ 2.93× too fast). C$MF3 = C$MF+C$MF2 = 768/frame → 15,750 u/s (795, verified). Ours: ENEMY_SPEED = 10,000 (state.ts:210), a single constant |vel| applied along a homing heading (sim.ts:1143) — ≈4.8% under the C$MF2 rate but STRUCTURALLY different: the ROM speed is quantised and rebuilt each frame from the active move-flag combination and rotating orientation (CPUMVL zeroes M$V* then re-adds per flag, WSCPU.MAC:762-789), varying step-to-step; ours is one fixed scalar. Magnitude roughly faithful, mechanism divergent. Class DIVERGENCE / accept correct. Numbers all check out — no transcription error, no radix error.
